_meta.subtaskTitle: Study belief in the Last Day (al-Yawm al-Akhir)
_meta.parentTaskTitle: Study the six pillars of Iman
_meta.claudeScholarQuery: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Study belief in the Last Day (al-Yawm al-Akhir)' under the parent task 'Study the six pillars of Iman' (module: faith_shahada_core), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus: (1) Quran-biased; (2) Hadith-biased.

muslimScholarQueries.query: Quran-biased query for belief in the Last Day
Hadith-biased query for belief in the Last Day

sources.translation: Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the east or the west, but [true] righteousness is [in] one who believes in God, the Last Day, the angels, the Book, and the prophets...
sources.rationale: The foundational Quranic definition of righteousness embeds belief in the Last Day as a core article of faith — anchors the subtask directly. Ref-confirmed by MS chunk '177. Righteousness is not that you turn your'. Arabic omitted: MS did not return verse-level Arabic.

notebookTrace.query: Quran-biased query for belief in the Last Day

sources.translation: Those who disbelieve have claimed that they will never be resurrected. Say, 'Yes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected; then you will surely be informed of what you did. And that, for God, is easy.'
sources.rationale: Directly addresses and affirms the reality of Resurrection — the core content of belief in al-Yawm al-Akhir. MS answer cites 64:7 verbatim and names it as anchor. Arabic omitted per MS disclaimer pattern.

notebookTrace.query: Quran-biased query for belief in the Last Day

sources.ref: Sahih Muslim, Book 1, Number 0005 (Hadith of Jibril — Umar ibn al-Khattab)
sources.translation: That you affirm your faith in Allah, in His angels, in His Books, in His Apostles, in the Day of Judgment, and you affirm your faith in the Divine Decree about good and evil.
sources.rationale: Hadith of Jibril — canonical definition of Iman explicitly names 'the Day of Judgment' (al-Yawm al-Akhir) as a pillar. Ref confirmed and matn quoted in MS answer.

notebookTrace.query: Hadith-biased query for belief in the Last Day

sources.ref: Sahih al-Bukhari, Volume 1, Book 2, Number 47 (Narrated Abu Huraira)
sources.translation: Faith is to believe in Allah, His angels, (the) meeting with Him, His Apostles, and to believe in Resurrection.
sources.rationale: Companion Gabriel-narration — names 'meeting with Him' and 'Resurrection' explicitly, the two axes of al-Yawm al-Akhir. Canonical ref confirmed by MS chunk.

notebookTrace.query: Hadith-biased query for belief in the Last Day

_groundedBarCheck.reason: 4 entries, all Bayyinah and direct. Bar met on both axes. 22:7 candidate dropped — answer paraphrased it but reference chunks did not quote the ayah body itself (only '177. Righteousness...', 64:7 phrase); stuck to ref-confirmed bindings.
